articles.title: IMD forecasts rain and gusty winds across North India; yellow alert issued for Delhi
articles.description: Moderate to light rains are expected in Punjab, Haryana, Chandigarh, and Delhi from May 12 (Tuesday) to May 15 (Thursday); in West Uttar Pradesh, East Rajasthan on May 12 (Tuesday); and in East Uttar Pradesh on May 12 (Tuesday) and May 13 (Wednesday).

articles.title: Bangladeshi national gets three-year imprisonment for forged Indian passport by UP court
articles.description: A Bangladeshi individual, Abdul Amin, has received a three-year jail term from a Ballia court for his involvement in a serious forgery case. By using fake Indian documents, he managed to acquire a phony passport and illegally claim land in West Bengal. With roots in a Rohingya camp, Amin has been known to travel internationally with these forged credentials.

articles.title: Rajasthan Lab Assistant answer key 2026 expected to be released soon at rssb.rajasthan.gov.in: Direct link to download here
articles.description: The Rajasthan Staff Selection Board is expected to release the Rajasthan Lab Assistant Answer Key 2026 soon after conducting the recruitment exam on May 9 and 10. Around seven lakh candidates appeared for the examination held for 804 vacancies. The answer key, response sheets, and question papers will help aspirants estimate their scores before the final results are announced.

articles.title: Who Is Kamla Persad-Bissessar? Bihar-Linked Trinidad PM Goes Viral For ‘Coolie Girl’ Remark: ‘What An Incredible Journey’
articles.description: Trinidad and Tobago PM Kamla Persad-Bissessar’s remarks about the word “coolie” have sparked debate online, with many discussing identity and history

articles.title: Ex-investigation officer who took statements over the phone gets jail for forgery
articles.description: The defence said the investigation officer was experiencing grief from his father's sudden death, compounded by accumulated case backlogs and mounting work pressures.

articles.title: New India Assur Standalone March 2026 Net Sales at Rs 12,487.60 crore, up 13.88% Y-o-Y
Abhishek Bachchan shuts fee rumours on Raja Shivaji; calls Riteish Deshmukh’s film sacred project
articles.description: Abhishek Bachchan has opened up about joining Raja Shivaji without even reading the script, shutting down reports of hefty actor fees. Praising Riteish Deshmukh and Genelia Deshmukh, Abhishek called the film a “sacred” passion project and said it was never about money but an “emotional transaction,” with his heartfelt comments now winning praise across social media.

Hantavirus-hit cruise ship leaves Tenerife as WHO completes evacuation operation
articles.description: The hantavirus-stricken MV Hondius has departed Tenerife for the Netherlands after passengers and crew were evacuated. The operation repatriated 94 individuals, with three deaths reported since the outbreak began nine days ago. Health officials emphasize the low risk to the public, as the virus rarely spreads person-to-person.

articles.title: Trump all set for China trip, eyes 'great things' for both countries
articles.description: President Trump expressed optimism for his upcoming visit to China and meeting with President Xi Jinping, anticipating positive outcomes for both nations. Accompanied by prominent business leaders, Trump aims to discuss trade, Iran, tariffs, and AI, seeking to strengthen US economic and national security interests.

articles.title: Petrol, Diesel Prices Hiked On May 12? Check Updated City-Wise Fuel Rates
articles.description: Indian state refiners are expecting a modest increase in retail fuel prices within days, as the government seeks to balance the impact of the ten-week war in the Persian Gulf and the needs of...

articles.title: Karnal/ Rohtak: Strike by sanitation workers’ leaves cities choked with garbage
articles.description: The workers’ bodies on Monday took out marches in their cities and submitted memorandums to local MLAs and mayors.

articles.title: File separate pleas on SIR’s impact on West Bengal polls: Supreme Court tells TMC
articles.description: A bench of Chief Justice of India Surya Kant and justice Joymalya Bagchi was hearing a batch of pleas challenging the SIR exercise in West Bengal.

articles.description: Dean David Hinks said he could not be more grateful to Anil and Marilyn for the extraordinary investment in the college's newest alumni.

articles.description: Tamil Nadu CM Thalapathy Vijay's cousin Pallavi Surendar recently opened up about the freshness TVK Chief brings as a politician. On the other hand, Keerthana Sundar mentioned what made people connect with him.

articles.title: Liquor remarks: War of words erupt between Omar, Iltija
articles.description: Omar on Sunday had said that people consume alcohol by their own will and government was not forcing anyone to visit liquor shops.

articles.description: International Nurses Day 2026 celebrates healthcare heroes with the theme “Our Nurses. Our Future. Empowered Nurses Save Lives.” Explore inspiring quotes and shareable images.

articles.title: More than 5 lakh crore defence projects approved carry focus on indigenisation: Air Marshal Ashutosh Dixit
articles.description: Air Marshal Ashutosh Dixit marks Operation Sindoor anniversary, highlighting Rs 5 lakh crore in defence projects and India's surge in indigenous manufacturing.

articles.title: Jailer tries to stop quarrel, attacked by inmates in Karnataka
articles.description: Jailer V.D. Kumbara sustained a hand fracture after being attacked by inmates while attempting to stop a brawl at the Jamakhandi prison in Bagalkot.

articles.title: Can Reliance Jio's fresh-issue play restore faith in India's billion-dollar IPOs?
articles.description: Reliance Jio may change its IPO plan. Instead of existing investors selling shares, it could be a fresh issue. This means money goes directly into Jio Platforms for debt reduction and expansion. This move could restore investor faith in large Indian IPOs. It might set a new standard for future mega listings. The market will watch this development closely.

articles.title: Delhi CM begins 5-day ‘seva and rescue’ campaign for homeless
articles.description: Delhi's Chief Minister launched a five-day "Seva and Rescue Campaign" to assist over 300 sick and homeless individuals, providing shelter and medical care.

articles.title: Delhi mandates thicker green nets at C&D sites
articles.description: Delhi mandates construction sites to install green nets with 100 GSM thickness to curb dust pollution, enhancing compliance and monitoring efforts.

articles.title: Deadly Hantavirus Cruise Ship Outbreak: List Of Countries That Have Reported Cases
articles.description: What began as a cruise through South Atlantic has now turned into a multinational health scare, with hantavirus cases linked to the MV Hondius reported across Europe and the US.

articles.description: NIA joins probe after gelatine sticks found on PM's convoy route

articles.title: AIIMS-led trial finds low-cost drug digoxin may improve outcomes in rheumatic heart disease
articles.description: A trial by AIIMS shows digoxin may reduce worsening heart failure in rheumatic heart disease patients, offering a low-cost treatment option.

articles.title: Bengal to give B’desh border fence land to MHA in 45 days
articles.description: Kolkata: CM Suvendu Adhikari on Monday announced that his govt has initiated the process of transferring land to Union ministry of home affairs (MHA) .

articles.title: Homestays in and around Hampi inspected
articles.description: Vijayanagara SP S Jahnavi leads a major inspection of Hampi homestays. Out of 133 units, 132 are operating without mandatory Tourism Department registration.

articles.title: Show us a single project not opposed by environmentalists, green lobby: Supreme Court
articles.description: The Supreme Court refused to entertain a plea challenging the environmental clearance for Pipavav port expansion in Gujarat, questioning environmentalists' stance on development projects. The court emphasized the necessity of infrastructure for national development, while allowing the environmentalist to approach the NGT again with specific objections.

articles.title: World’s longest wild snake in Indonesia: The 23-foot ‘Baroness’ that stunned scientists and raised conservation concerns
articles.description: Rest of World News: At 23 feet 8 inches long, a wild snake in Indonesia has quietly stepped into global attention. Locals call her “The Baroness”, but her registered name.

articles.title: Elon Musk, Tim Cook to join Trump on China visit amid US–China trade tensions
articles.description: Tesla CEO Elon Musk and Apple chief Tim Cook will accompany US President Donald Trump on his China visit this week for talks with President Xi Jinping

articles.title: Cambridge Shooting: Multiple People Shot on Memorial Drive, Massachusetts
articles.description: Officials said a 20-year-old male suspect was shot by state police during the confrontation and was taken down at the scene.

articles.title: Academic excellence rewarded: Gumla dominates Jharkhand board exams; Students and teachers celebrated for outstanding results
articles.description: In a stunning display of academic prowess, Gumla district has emerged as a leader in the Jharkhand JAC board examinations. Students from Class X and XII achieved outstanding results, with numerous individuals claiming top spots and impressively high scores.

articles.title: Tulsa Fire: Video Shows Massive Fire, Heavy Smoke At HF Sinclair Refinery In Oklahoma
articles.description: A major fire broke out at the HF Sinclair refinery in Tulsa, sending heavy black smoke and flames into the air.Tulsa and Berryhill firefighters responded for standby support, but the refinery’s own fire brigade brought the blaze under control.

articles.title: Psychology Says This Simple Habit Makes You Instantly More Magnetic
articles.description: Genuine curiosity is psychology's secret to magnetism. Asking real, thoughtful questions makes others feel interesting and seen, triggering a dopamine response that draws people toward you more powerfully than charm or impressiveness ever could.

articles.title: New Delhi to host first International Big Cat Alliance summit in June, global conservation push planned
articles.description: New Delhi will host the first International Big Cat Alliance summit on June 1 and 2. World leaders and experts will gather to finalize the 'Delhi Declaration'. This document will guide global cooperation for conserving tigers, lions, leopards, jaguars, pumas, cheetahs, and snow leopards. The summit will focus on best practices and policy synergy for habitat protection.

articles.title: Delhi Capitals Stay Alive In Playoffs Race, Stun Punjab Kings In 211-Run Chase
articles.description: Delhi Capitals pull off a brilliant chase of 211 after being 33/3, defeating Punjab Kings in the penultimate over to keep their IPL 2026 playoff hopes alive.

articles.title: JMM takes a dig on PM’s appeal on cutting fuel use, other curbs
articles.description: The Jharkhand Mukti Morcha (JMM) has thrown shade on Prime Minister Modi's recent plea for fuel conservation, pointing out the contradiction posed by the BJP's elaborate campaign events in Gujarat. The party accused the central government of deceiving the public with unfulfilled promises, thereby fueling economic challenges, skyrocketing prices, and job scarcity.

articles.title: Chain collision injuries rise: Dozen injured in multiple vehicle collision in Danua Valley; Over-speeding cited as probable cause
articles.description: Late Sunday, a chaotic scene unfolded on NH-2's notorious Danua Valley as a multi-vehicle collision left a dozen people injured. The crash, involving six cars en route from Jharkhand to Bihar, led to a significant highway gridlock. Authorities acted quickly, ensuring that those injured received urgent medical care on-site, with some being sent to Hazaribag for more serious injuries.

articles.title: Federalism in the bin: Why India’s waste crisis cannot be solved by central decree
articles.description: Under the new Solid Waste Management Rules, 2026, came into effect from April 1, States and rural bodies in India shrink to mere executors of policy pushed by the Centre, which would prove ineffective in managing the waste crisis that has become a national ecological emergency

articles.title: VB-G RAM G Act to come into force from July 1: All you need to know
articles.description: The Centre on Monday announced that the Viksit Bharat — Guarantee for Rozgar and Ajeevika Mission (Gramin) Act will come into force across the country from July 1, replacing the Mahatma Gandhi National Rural Employment Guarantee Act (MGNREGA).

articles.title: 'Unknown Object Closing In': NASA Astronauts First UFO Scare On Cam? Gemini 7 Crew & A ‘Bogey’ Call
articles.description: "We have a bogey." Newly declassified Pentagon files have brought a 60-year-old space mystery back to life. During the 1965 Gemini 7 mission, astronauts Frank Borman and Jim Lovell reported a "brilliant body" shadowed by trillions of particles. Despite Houston claiming it was just their own booster rocket, the crew insisted they were seeing something else. Now, with 162 unsealed UAP files released to the public, the world is finally hearing the full story of what NASA's pioneers saw in the void.

articles.title: Young singer Sai Vignesh makes a mark with voice and choice
articles.description: Rare Carnatic compositions were presented by Sai Vignesh, who performed at Mudhra’s Chithirai Music Festival, showcasing creativity and classical depth

articles.title: ‘I will neither confirm nor deny’: Rajeev Chandrasekhar’s sarcastic take on Kerala CM race
articles.description: BJP Kerala chief Rajeev Chandrasekhar’s sarcastic social media post on KC Venugopal has intensified political buzz amid the Congress delay in announcing Kerala’s next chief minister.

articles.title: Mango farmers in Krishnagiri petition for tripartite meeting for price fixing
articles.description: Krishnagiri mango farmers petition for a tripartite meeting to secure fair pricing with the pulp industry and government support.

articles.title: After Mamata Banerjee's "Unity Call", BJP's "She's Calling Maoists" Charge
articles.description: The BJP said Mamata Banerjee's Trinamool is working to revive Maoists and will bring Maoists in the name of unity

articles.title: Census training begins in Karaikal ahead of 2026-27 population enumeration
articles.description: Census training for enumerators in Karaikal begins ahead of the 2026-27 population enumeration, running from May 11-13.

articles.title: Angamaly Urban Cooperative Society scam: investors threaten to intensify protest
articles.description: Investors in the Angamaly Urban Cooperative Society threaten protests amid a ₹96-crore scam investigation and ongoing financial struggles.

articles.title: Perfect Crown actress Lee Yeon reveals her driving scene was a CGI - here's why
articles.description: Perfect Crown actress Lee Yeon recently revealed on The Manager that her driver’s license was revoked after she missed the renewal deadline. She also surprised viewers by admitting that her driving scenes in the drama were completed using CGI because she no longer drives.
